title: A square waveform with crisp 90-degree corners. Precept enforces state as discrete positions — the stable plateaus are valid states; the instantaneous vertical edges are governed transitions; there is no analog slope or intermediate position, because the contract makes them structurally impossible.
conceptualBridge: The square wave means Precept enforces discrete state transitions because the step function's right-angle corners are architecturally impossible to blur — no gradient between states exists.
decision: Shortlisted: avg 4.5, no hard fails — the step-function waveform is the only candidate in this visual family; the crisp right-angle transitions are a precise analog of Precept's discrete state enforcement; maximum uniqueness score.
